title: Detecting Bridges in a Spanning Tree: Fix the Low Link Values

statement.markdown:
Given a graph represented as adjacency list, your task is to detect bridges in the graph using DFS.

A bridge is an edge that, when removed, increases the number of connected components in the graph.

You will receive the graph as a dictionary where each key is a node and its corresponding value is a list of its neighboring nodes.

You should return a list of edges that forms a bridge in the graph.
statement.inputFormat: Dictionary representing the graph where each key is a node and its corresponding value is a list of its neighboring nodes.
statement.outputFormat: List of edges that forms a bridge in the graph.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(V+E), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges. Note that the graph may not be connected.

examples.explanation: The graph has four nodes and four edges. All edges form a bridge in this graph.

hints.text: Use DFS to traverse the graph. Keep track of low values for each node and the discovery time for each node.

editorial.title: Detecting Bridges in a Spanning Tree
editorial.intuition: The low value of a node represents the smallest discovery time reachable from the subtree rooted at the node.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use DFS to traverse the graph. Keep track of low values for each node and the discovery time for each node. Update low values whenever a bridge edge is found.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: std::vector<std::vector<int> > bridges; void dfs(int node, int parent, int discoveryTime, int lowValue, std::vector<int> &parentNodes, int &timer); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public List<List<Integer>> bridges = new ArrayList<>(); public void dfs(int node, int parent, int discoveryTime, int lowValue, List<Integer> parentNodes, int timer) {} };
starterCode.python: class Solution: def __init__(self): self.bridges = [] def dfs(self, node, parent, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer): pass
starterCode.javascript: function solve(graph) { let bridges = []; function dfs(node, parent, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer) {} }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: std::vector<std::vector<int> > bridges; void dfs(int node, int parent, int discoveryTime, int lowValue, std::vector<int> &parentNodes, int &timer, bool isParentVisited) { if (parent == -1) { discoveryTime = timer++; lowValue = timer++; } parentNodes[node] = parent; for (int neighbor : graph[node]) { if (!visited[neighbor]) { visited[neighbor] = true; dfs(neighbor, node, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, isParentVisited); } else if (!isParentVisited) { lowValue = std::min(lowValue, visited[neighbor]); } } } }; void Solution::solve(vector<vector<int>> &graph) { visited.clear(); visited.resize(graph.size(), false); bridges.clear(); int timer = 0; vector<int> parentNodes(graph.size(), -1); dfs(0, -1, 0, 0, parentNodes, timer, false); }
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public List<List<Integer>> bridges; public void dfs(int node, int parent, int discoveryTime, int lowValue, List<Integer> parentNodes, int timer, boolean isParentVisited) { if (parent == -1) { discoveryTime = timer++; lowValue = timer++; } parentNodes.set(node, parent); for (int neighbor : graph.get(node)) { if (!visited.get(neighbor)) { visited.set(neighbor, true); dfs(neighbor, node, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, isParentVisited); } else if (!isParentVisited) { lowValue = Math.min(lowValue, visited.get(neighbor)); } } } }; public void Solution::solve(List<List<Integer>> graph) { visited.clear(); visited.resize(graph.size(), false); bridges.clear(); int timer = 0; List<Integer> parentNodes = new ArrayList<>(); parentNodes.addAll(Collections.nCopies(graph.size(), -1)); dfs(0, -1, 0, 0, parentNodes, timer, false); }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def __init__(self): self.bridges = [] def dfs(self, node, parent, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, isParentVisited): if parent == -1: discoveryTime = timer lowValue = timer parentNodes[node] = parent for neighbor in self.graph[node]: if not self.visited[neighbor]: self.visited[neighbor] = True self.dfs(neighbor, node, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, isParentVisited) elif not isParentVisited: lowValue = min(lowValue, self.visited[neighbor]) def solve(self, graph): self.visited = [False] * len(graph) self.bridges = [] timer = 0 parentNodes = [-1] * len(graph) self.dfs(0, -1, 0, 0, parentNodes, timer, False)
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(graph) { let bridges = []; function dfs(node, parent, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, isParentVisited) { if (parent == -1) { discoveryTime = timer++; lowValue = timer++; } parentNodes[node] = parent; for (let neighbor of graph[node]) { if (!visited[neighbor]) { visited[neighbor] = true; dfs(neighbor, node, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, isParentVisited); } else if (!isParentVisited) { lowValue = Math.min(lowValue, visited[neighbor]); } } } let visited = new Array(graph.length).fill(false); timer = 0; let parentNodes = new Array(graph.length).fill(-1); dfs(0, -1, 0, 0, parentNodes, timer, false); return bridges; }

functionSignature.cpp: bool dfs(int node, int parent, int &discoveryTime, int &lowValue, std::vector<int> &parentNodes, bool &isParentVisited); void solve(std::vector<std::vector<int>> &graph);
functionSignature.java: public void dfs(int node, int parent, int discoveryTime, int lowValue, List<Integer> parentNodes, boolean isParentVisited); public void solve(List<List<Integer>> graph);
functionSignature.python: def dfs(self, node, parent, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, isParentVisited); def solve(self, graph):
functionSignature.javascript: function dfs(node, parent, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, isParentVisited); function solve(graph);

solutions.python: class Solution: def __init__(self): self.bridges = [] def dfs(self, node, parent, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, isParentVisited): if parent == -1: discoveryTime = timer lowValue = timer + 1 parentNodes[node] = parent for neighbor in self.graph[node]: if not self.visited[neighbor]: self.visited[neighbor] = True self.dfs(neighbor, node, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, True) elif not isParentVisited: lowValue = min(lowValue, self.visited[neighbor]) def solve(self, graph): self.visited = [False] * len(graph) timer = 0 parentNodes = [-1] * len(graph) for i in range(len(graph)): if not self.visited[i]: self.dfs(i, -1, 0, 0, parentNodes, timer, False) return [[node, parentNodes[node]] for node in range(len(parentNodes)) if parentNodes[node] == -1]
solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: void dfs(int node, int parent, int &discoveryTime, int &lowValue, std::vector<int> &parentNodes, int &timer, bool &isParentVisited) { if (parent == -1) { discoveryTime = timer++; lowValue = timer++; } parentNodes[node] = parent; for (int neighbor : graph[node]) { if (!visited[neighbor]) { visited[neighbor] = true; dfs(neighbor, node, discoveryTime, lowValue, parentNodes, timer, true); } else if (!isParentVisited) { lowValue = std::min(lowValue, visited[neighbor]); } } } public: std::vector<std::vector<int> > solve(std::vector<std::vector<int>> graph) { visited.clear(); visited.resize(graph.size(), false); int timer = 0; std::vector<int> parentNodes(graph.size(), -1); for (int i = 0; i < graph.size(); i++) { if (!visited[i]) { dfs(i, -1, timer, timer, parentNodes, timer, false); } } std::vector<std::vector<int>> result; for (int i = 0; i < parentNodes.size(); i++) { if (parentNodes[i] == -1) { result.push_back({i, -1}); } } return result; } };
